question_title: Will the Bank of England report more than 60000 net mortgage approvals for house purchase in the UK for February 2026?
summary: UK net mortgage approvals for house purchase need to exceed 60,000 for February 2026. The data hasn't been officially released yet (BoE page says "to be published at 9.30am"), expected late March/early April. January 2026 was 59,999 — essentially right at the threshold. The MQL5 economic calendar shows 60,141 as either a consensus forecast or possibly an already-released actual value. The distribution center for February sits almost exactly on the 60,000 threshold. A Monte Carlo model using weighted scenarios (consensus, optimism-bias, seasonal-bounce) yields P(>60,000) ≈ 51%. Adding a 15% probability that the MQL5 figure represents the actual released value (60,141 > 60,000) lifts the overall estimate to ~55%. The clear 4-month downward trend (65,900→59,999) is offset by potential seasonal recovery and favorable pre-conflict conditions in February.

factors.description: Consensus forecast of ~60,141 is barely above the 60,000 threshold - only 141 above, essentially at the knife edge
MQL5 economic calendar shows 60,141 as 'Last release Actual' with Previous=59,999 - could be the actual released February value OR a forecast estimate
Clear downward trend from Sep 2025 (65,900) to Jan 2026 (59,999) - 5,900 decline over 4 months suggesting continued weakness
Jan→Feb seasonal pattern shows potential for bounce: Feb 2024 was +4,300 from Jan, though Feb 2025 was -600 from Jan
January 2026 missed consensus by 2,000 (59,999 vs 62,000), but September 2025 beat consensus by 1,900 (65,900 vs 64,000) - no clear systematic optimism bias
Pre-conflict conditions in February 2026 were positive: mortgage rates declining, rate cut expectations, broker optimism for H1 2026 uptick
Iran conflict started Feb 28 - minimal impact on February approvals data since it's at very end of month
6-month trailing average of 64,100 is well above 60,000, suggesting January's 59,999 may be a trough rather than new normal

reasoning:
**Forecast: 0.55 (55% probability YES)**

Key reasoning:
- The February 2026 BoE Money and Credit data hasn't been officially released yet (expected late March/early April)
- The market consensus estimate from MQL5 is ~60,141 — only 141 above the 60,000 threshold
- January 2026 was 59,999 (essentially at threshold), after a 4-month decline from 65,900
- Monte Carlo simulation with weighted scenarios yields P(>60,000) ≈ 51-55%
- The slight lean toward YES (vs 50%) comes from: (a) consensus being marginally above threshold, (b) potential seasonal Jan→Feb recovery, (c) favorable pre-conflict conditions in February, and (d) small probability (~15%) that MQL5's 60,141 is the already-released actual value
sources_consulted: [Money and Credit - February 2026 (to be published at 9.30am](https://www.bankofengland.co.uk/statistics/money-and-credit/2026/february-2026)
[Bank of England Money and Credit Report - January 2026 | Estate Agent Networking](https://estateagentnetworking.co.uk/bank-of-england-money-and-credit-report-january-2026)
[BoE Mortgage Approvals 2026 - economic index from the United Kingdom](https://www.mql5.com/en/economic-calendar/united-kingdom/boe-mortgage-approvals)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves to yes if the Bank of England's Money and Credit release for February 2026 reports net mortgage approvals for house purchase strictly above 60000. It resolves to no otherwise. The primary source is the release page "Money and Credit - February 2026" at https://www.bankofengland.co.uk/statistics/money-and-credit/2026/february-2026
fine_print: Use net mortgage approvals for house purchase for February 2026, not remortgaging approvals, not mortgage lending flows, and not March 2026 data. Ignore later revisions unless the Bank of England replaces the original release before resolution. Candidate Sources: Bank of England statistics release dates page at https://www.bankofengland.co.uk/statistics | Bank of England statistical releases page at https://www.bankofengland.co.uk/news/statistics
